title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. barrelieri
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. barrelieri, also known as the common daisy or Barrelier's daisy, is a subspecies of the daisy plant, characterized by its white petals and yellow center. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. barrelieri include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. barrelieri can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
